title: Two Directions at Once

mood.label: Uneasy calm 🌥️
mood.summary: The Gulf kept cooling and oil slid toward calm while a prime minister fell, an Ebola count crossed a thousand and the heat bore down — a day that pulled both ways and settled just under the line.

style.genre: vibraphone-led darkwave / dub-trip-hop — a sampled vibraphone tracing a circling minor topline over a slow, dub-spaced AkaiLinn kit with deep tape-echo, a soft sine sub and a low bowed-string darkwave pad underneath, with sparse muted-guitar glints; F melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh lifting the minor third just enough to keep the calm honest and unsettled — neither rising nor sinking

sampleSources: Strudel default library — VCSL vibraphone & strings, AkaiLinn drum machine (preloaded)
pattern: stack( s("~ rim ~ ~, bd ~ ~ ~ bd ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ ~ hh ~ ~ hh ~").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(0.62).room(0.32).rsize(4).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.5).delayfeedback(0.42).hpf(180), note("<[f3,af3,c4,e4] [bf2,d3,f3,af3] [c3,e3,g3,bf3] [af2,c3,ef3,g3]>").s("vibraphone").attack(0.01).release(0.9).gain(0.5).lpf(sine.range(900,2400).slow(16)).room(0.4).rsize(5).pan(0.42), note("<[c3,f3,af3] [d3,f3,bf3] [bf2,e3,g3] [c3,ef3,af3]>").s("sawtooth").attack(1.6).sustain(0.8).release(3).lpf(sine.range(360,820).slow(24)).gain(0.2).room(0.55).rsize(6), note("f1 ~ ~ f1 ~ ~ c2 ~").s("sine").lpf(220).gain(0.5).attack(0.02).release(0.5), note("~ c5 ~ af4 ~ e5 d5 ~ ~ bf4 ~ ~ c5 ~ af4 ~").s("vibraphone").gain(0.34).lpf(3200).delay(0.34).delaytime(0.375).delayfeedback(0.4).room(0.45).pan(0.58).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.18).pan(0.3)), s("~ ~ gtr ~ ~ ~ ~ ~").gain(0.18).speed(1.5).lpf(2600).room(0.5).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.666).hpf(400) )

lines: The strait stayed open, the tankers came through,
a price on a barrel slid down to seventy-two-and-some.
Somewhere a ceasefire learned how to hold its breath,
and the radio called it a good morning, almost.
Two directions at once and the needle hangs just under the line —
one door easing open, another quietly closing for good.
Don't tell me it's bright, don't tell me it's over,
tell me the truth: it's a grey-cloud day, and we're learning to stand in it.
A prime minister sets down the keys in the hall,
a fever count crosses a thousand a continent away.
The south of the map climbs toward forty-five,
and an old man who steered the money is gone at a hundred years.
Two directions at once and the needle hangs just under the line —
vultures coming back, a city breathing a little more clean.
Don't tell me it's bright, don't tell me it's broken,
tell me the truth: it's a grey-cloud day, and we're learning to stand in it.
